# Blossom — Full Reference

> Blossom is a SaaS platform for analyzing viral social media content. It fetches videos from Instagram and TikTok, runs multi-stage AI analysis using Google Gemini, classifies hooks/formats/tactics, surfaces trends and improvement suggestions, and supports analysis versioning to track content improvements across iterations.

This file is a single-fetch reference intended for LLMs and AI assistants. Last updated: 2026-05-19.

---

## Product overview

Blossom helps creators, agencies, and brands understand what makes social media content go viral. Users paste a TikTok or Instagram URL (or upload a video) and Blossom returns:

- A virality score (0–100) per platform
- The hook style (e.g. "Direct Question", "Bold Claim", "Storytime opener")
- The format class (e.g. "Talking Head", "Tutorial", "Day in the Life")
- The tactics used (e.g. "fast cuts", "text overlay", "audio drop sync")
- Music and audio metadata (when applicable)
- Specific suggestions to improve the content for higher virality

---

<a id="public-api"></a>
## Public API Reference

Base URL: `https://blossai.com/api/v1`

### Authentication

All endpoints require an API key. Send it as the `X-API-Key` header:

```
X-API-Key: blsm_<40-hex-characters>
```

API keys are managed inside Account → API Keys (requires platin or admin plan). Keys follow the format `blsm_` + 40 hex characters and are hashed (SHA-256) before storage — raw keys are shown only once at creation.

API keys can be scoped to specific category IDs. A scoped key only returns data for its assigned categories.

### Rate Limits

| Plan  | Limit             |
|-------|-------------------|
| platin| 100 requests/min  |
| admin | 600 requests/min  |

Exceeding the limit returns `429 Too Many Requests`.

### Endpoints

#### `GET /videos`
Returns analyzed videos from the library.

Query parameters:
- `limit` (int, default 20, max 100)
- `offset` (int)
- `platform` (`instagram` | `tiktok`)
- `influencer_id` (int)
- `min_score` (int 0–100)
- `format_class_id` (int)
- `hook_class_id` (int)

Response (excerpt):
```json
{
  "videos": [
    {
      "id": 123,
      "platform": "instagram",
      "url": "https://...",
      "thumbnail_url": "https://...",
      "caption": "...",
      "like_count": 45000,
      "view_count": 1200000,
      "overall_score": 82,
      "instagram_score": 79,
      "tiktok_score": 85,
      "format_classes": ["Talking Head", "Tutorial"],
      "hook_classes": ["Direct Question"],
      "tactics": ["Fast cuts", "Text overlay"],
      "created_at": "2026-03-01T00:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "total": 1500,
  "limit": 20,
  "offset": 0
}
```

#### `GET /influencers`
Returns influencer profiles. Query parameters: `limit`, `offset`, `platform`, `tier` (`nano|micro|mid|macro|mega`), `min_followers`.

#### `GET /hooks`
Hook classifications and patterns.

#### `GET /formats`
Format taxonomy.

#### `GET /tactics`
Tactic library.

#### `GET /music`
Music and audio metadata.

#### `GET /suggestions`
Content improvement suggestions.

#### `GET /trending`
Trending content aggregation.

#### `GET /categories`
Category filters.

For up-to-date full schemas, see [public-api.md](https://github.com/) in the repository docs.

---

<a id="architecture"></a>
## Architecture

Blossom is a monorepo with two main workspaces.

```
blossom/
├── backend/    Express + TypeScript API (port 3001)
├── frontend/   React 19 + Vite 6 SPA (port 5173)
└── docs/       Feature documentation
```

### Tech stack
| Layer       | Technology                                |
|-------------|-------------------------------------------|
| Frontend    | React 19, Vite 6, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS|
| Backend     | Express 4, TypeScript 5.7                 |
| Database    | Supabase (PostgreSQL 15)                  |
| Auth        | Supabase Auth (JWT)                       |
| AI          | Google Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro)            |
| Real-time   | Socket.IO 4                               |
| Billing     | Paddle                                    |
| Social data | HikerAPI (Instagram), LamaTok (TikTok)    |
| Media       | FFmpeg, Meyda (audio analysis)            |

### Backend layout (post-refactor Feb 2026)
- `routes/analysis/` — 12 sub-routers, barrel index.ts
- `routes/social/` — 5 sub-routers, barrel index.ts
- `routes/public-api/` — 9 sub-routers (videos, influencers, formats, hooks, tactics, music, suggestions, trending, categories)
- `lib/gemini/` — 6 modules, barrel index.ts
- `services/` — 6 service files (media-helpers, progress-state, video-fetch-service, video-analysis-service, class-management-service, content-analysis-service)
- Layered: routes → services → lib → config (strictly one-way, no circular deps)

### Frontend layout
- Entry: `src/main.tsx` wraps App in providers
- `src/App.tsx` — react-router-dom v7 route definitions
- `src/context/` — AuthContext, ImpersonationContext
- `src/lib/api.ts` — fetch wrapper for `/api/*`
- `src/lib/supabase.ts` — Supabase browser client
- `src/lib/socket.ts` — Socket.IO client singleton

---

<a id="content-analysis-pipeline"></a>
## Content Analysis Pipeline

When a user submits a video, the pipeline runs in stages:

1. **Fetch** — backend pulls metadata (caption, like_count, view_count, thumbnail) from HikerAPI (Instagram) or LamaTok (TikTok). Since May 2026, raw video files are NOT downloaded — `content_videos.local_video_path` stays NULL and the player redirects to the source URL on play.

2. **Classify** — multi-stage Gemini calls assign:
   - Hook class (from `viral_hook_classes`)
   - Format class (from `viral_format_classes`)
   - Tactics (from `viral_tactics`)
   These flow through `classifyAndLink` and `processTacticsFromAnalysis` in `services/video-analysis-service.ts`.

3. **Score** — virality scores per platform (`overall_score`, `instagram_score`, `tiktok_score`) computed from engagement metrics.

4. **Audio analysis** (when applicable) — `audio-analyzer.ts` extracts beats, drops, energy curves, and section boundaries using Meyda for energy/spectral features and fluent-ffmpeg for extraction. Music metadata is joined from `content_music` via `content_video_music`.

5. **Suggestions** — Gemini produces actionable improvement suggestions based on the analysis and trending patterns.

6. **Persist** — results stored in `content_analysis`, linked to `content_videos` and category-scoped through `content_video_classes`.

Real-time progress is broadcast via Socket.IO to the originating user.

### Versioning
The pipeline supports analysis versioning to track improvements across iterations. Users can compare two versions of an analysis side-by-side.

---

## Authentication & Authorization

- End users authenticate via Supabase Auth (email/password or OAuth). The backend trusts the Supabase JWT in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header for application API calls.
- Public API consumers (agencies, integrations) use the `X-API-Key` header — this avoids collision with the user JWT scheme.
- Plans: `free`, `plus`, `platin`, `admin`. API key management requires platin or admin.

---

## Audio Analysis Feature

`audio-analyzer.ts` extracts audio from video and produces:
- Beat positions and tempo
- Energy curve and peaks
- Section boundaries (intro / build / drop / outro)
- Spectral features (centroid, rolloff, spectralFlux*)

(*spectralFlux is a Meyda feature but not in the MeydaFeaturesObject TypeScript type — backend code casts to Record<string, any>.)

Music context for a video comes from the `content_music` table joined via `content_video_music`.

---

## Database tables (high level)

- `content_videos` — fetched videos
- `content_influencers` — profiles
- `content_uploads` — user-uploaded videos
- `content_analysis` — analysis result rows (versioned)
- `content_music`, `content_video_music` — music metadata + join
- `viral_format_classes`, `viral_hook_classes`, `viral_tactics` — taxonomy
- `content_video_classes`, `video_tactics` — joins between videos and taxonomy
- `profiles` — synced from Supabase auth
- `social_accounts`, `social_posts`, `social_metrics_history`, `social_post_metrics` — social-integration data
- `api_keys`, `api_key_usage`, `api_request_log` — public API plumbing (RLS enabled)
- `content_improvements`, `bulk_analysis_results`, `ai_cost_logs` — operational

---
